vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical reality. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are largely absent or in a state of terminal decline.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil surface appears barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil surface appears barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot + Rabbit): Systemic Loss: The Pothos is absent. Only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil surface appears barren.

plant_audit.P1: Empty. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty. No signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Empty. The Pothos has been removed or has completely decomposed.
plant_audit.P4: Empty. No signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_condition: The soil in all pots appears dark and potentially oversaturated or simply lacking organic cover. There is no evidence of moss or secondary growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) noted in the central pot area, likely an intervention attempt that failed to support plant life.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green sprout in the central pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-3: The sprout shows signs of chlorosis and postural collapse.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_current: The sprout has vanished. All pots are now effectively sterile/empty.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total collapse. The absence of any photosynthetic tissue suggests that the plants have either been removed or have died and been cleared away. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshell fragments suggests a human-led intervention to improve soil quality, which did not prevent the total loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image sequence. Initially, a single, struggling sprout was visible in the central pot. Over the course of the five-day observation period, this specimen underwent rapid senescence and eventual disappearance. As of the current timestamp, all pots are devoid of their registered botanical occupants. The biome is currently in a state of total systemic failure.
